scope: Compact inventory of MoonCat color-related sources and claims. This file records evidence levels and source claims; it does not define canonical hue ranges, exact palettes, formulas, rendering behavior, or per-cat mappings.

trustTiers.current-maintainer-technical: Current MoonCatRescue technical documentation or maintained source/code surface.
trustTiers.local-upstream-reference: Local upstream source snapshot registered in data/sources.json; reference input, not curated KB data.
trustTiers.verified-onchain-source-surface: Verified contract/source identity and compact function surface, without importing ABI/source internals.
trustTiers.high-confidence-maintainer-explanation: Maintainer-authored or user-supplied maintainer explanation; useful for interpretation but not source-code proof by itself.
trustTiers.official-publication-context: MoonCatRescue publication or article context; verify exact behavior against contract/source records before canonical import.
trustTiers.local-policy-or-summary: Repo-local policy, scaffold, or compact summary.

sources.title: ADR 0017 - MoonCat Color Math
extractedClaims: The original JavaScript mooncatparser script is treated by this ADR as canonical for true MoonCat color, including RGBToHSL output.
Hue values are truncated to integer values before use.
Twelve human-friendly color labels are defined as 30-degree divisions around the HSL color wheel in the ADR.
The ADR states pure-coated MoonCats ignore the coat-pattern color when determining visual clone/littermate matching because that color is not visually used.
The ADR notes MoonCatColors mapColors records edge cases where Solidity color math does not dynamically match JavaScript behavior.
sourceEvidence: Raw GitLab ADR fetched successfully on 2026-07-04.
ADR status is Accepted and updated 2025-09-17.
ADR lists integer hue ranges and explains JavaScript floating-point caveats.
limitations: This inventory records ADR claims but does not update data/color-hues.json.
Exact hue ranges and edge-case overrides should be promoted only in a separate color-model pass.
ADR claims should still be reconciled with local parser and on-chain source surfaces before curated canonical data changes.

sources.title: Local mooncatparser.js reference snapshot
extractedClaims: The local parser snapshot directly contains RGBToHSL, HSLToRGB, RGBToHex, and derivePalette helper names.
derivePalette reads HSL from RGB input and uses hue-like values to produce five palette entries.
The local parser snapshot has explicit special color arrays for black/white genesis-like branches before falling back to derivePalette.
sourceEvidence: references/upstream/mooncatrescue/mooncatparser.js lines around derivePalette show RGBToHSL and HSLToRGB helper use.
The parser snippet around the color branch shows derivePalette(r, g, b, invert) used for non-special cases.
No code body or large parser table is imported into this inventory.
limitations: The snapshot is a reference input, not normalized curated data.
This inventory does not copy source code, palette arrays, generated images, or parser internals.
The parser snapshot by itself does not define human-friendly hue labels.

sources.title: MoonCatColors contract surface
extractedClaims: Existing contract summaries record MoonCatColors as an on-chain color reference and palette-generation contract.
Existing function surfaces include RGBToHue, hueToRGB, deriveColors, mapColors, colorsOf, accessoryColorsOf, colorAlpha, paletteOf, hueName, and hueIntOf.
The registered LibMoonCat snapshot includes ABI surface evidence that colorsOf returns uint8[24] and accessoryColorsOf returns uint8[45].
sourceEvidence: data/contracts.json and data/contract-surfaces.json summarize MoonCatColors role and function names.
references/upstream/mooncatrescue/libmooncat-limited.js includes a MoonCatColors ABI entry with colorsOf and deriveColors surfaces.
No ABI JSON or source code is imported into this inventory.
limitations: No palette values, hue-name table, edge-case mappings, or source internals are imported.
Function names and output widths are surface evidence, not a full color model.

sources.title: MoonCats On-Chain Guide
extractedClaims: The article says each rescued MoonCat has one embedded color code that is extrapolated into a five-color main image palette plus accessory complement colors.
The article says MoonCatColors colorsOf returns 24 numbers: RGB values for 8 colors.
The article describes the order as glow/original color code, five base image colors, and two complimentary colors.
The article describes MoonCatSVGs as assembling SVG images on-demand from on-chain data.
sourceEvidence: Medium page lines for MoonCatColors describe colorsOf and the 24-number/8-color return shape.
Medium page lines for MoonCatSVGs describe imageOf as outputting SVG data as a text string.
limitations: Article claims should be checked against contract source/ABI before canonical data import.
The article does not provide exact RGB palettes or per-cat mappings in this inventory.

sources.title: Visual of the Hue-based color wheel; how MoonCats are colorized
extractedClaims: The post explains MoonCat image colorization using hue-wheel language and an HSV framing.
The post distinguishes standard and inverted palette examples in the visualization.
A follow-up comment says prior 30-degree color classification buckets should likely be shifted down 15 degrees to better match human perception.
A follow-up comment says black and white categories would be logical for genesis cats when bucketing by what the human eye sees.
sourceEvidence: Reddit page shows author MidnightLightning and the hue-wheel post title.
Reddit comments by MidnightLightning discuss HSV/hue wheel, standard/inverted palettes, shifted bucket boundaries, and genesis black/white categorization.
limitations: Reddit content is explanatory and visual-classification oriented.
Do not promote bucket boundaries or genesis categorization to canonical data from this source alone.
The post uses HSV wording, while source/parser/ADR language also uses HSL/HLS; terminology must be reconciled in a later color-model pass.

sources.title: MidnightLightning Discord color explanation, 2024-01-06
sources.authorOrOrigin: User-supplied Discord blurb attributed to MidnightLightning / MoonCatRescue Team
extractedClaims: The blurb says RGB color is converted to HSL and only hue is kept.
It says palette colors are derived with max saturation and fixed lightness values.
It says standard MoonCats use a spread of the same hue for border, pattern, fur, and belly, with nose/toes/ears shifted backward 40 degrees.
It says pale MoonCats use the palette differently, with the vibrant DNA/glow hue used as pattern and the base fur becoming the shifted color.
It says the accessory system adds two complementary colors opposite the wheel and on-chain metadata contracts expose 8 palette colors.
It says DNA/glow color is not visually used for all MoonCats, and pure-patterned MoonCats have a calculated pattern color that is not visually used.
sourceEvidence: The prompt for this run supplied the blurb summary and date.
No raw Discord URL, message export, or official mirror was reviewed in this pass.
limitations: This is not raw source-code proof.
Claims should be confirmed against ADR, parser, and on-chain source before changing canonical color data.
The original Discord text was not imported verbatim.

sources.title: mooncat_traits.json observed color fields
extractedClaims: data/color-hues.json records aggregate observed hueName, hueInt, pale, and genesis fields from the local 25,440-row snapshot.
Observed black and white hueInt values are recorded as non-degree special numeric values in data/color-hues.json.
Observed pale is a separate boolean field and should not be inferred from hueName alone.
sourceEvidence: data/color-hues.json version 2 records aggregate counts and limitations.
data/trait-index.json records schema metadata for hueInt, hueName, pale, and genesis fields.
limitations: This source does not define exact palettes, parser behavior, or per-cat mappings in curated KB data.
The full upstream trait table remains a reference snapshot, not imported curated data.

sources.title: Repo color math note
extractedClaims: docs/color-math.md says canonical MoonCat hue names, coat trait mappings, special coat categories, and pale-coat adjustments were still not fully verified in the repo at that point.
docs/color-math.md records circular hue-distance as a method note and warns that character-cat hue ranges are community-curated narrative/filter ranges.
docs/color-math.md says pale adjustment rules should remain experimental until source data and behavior are documented.
sourceEvidence: Local docs inspected during this pass.
The doc is used as repo-local status context rather than external proof.
limitations: This is a local status summary, not a primary color source.
It should be superseded by source-backed data if later color-model work promotes verified claims.

claimIndex.notes: ADR and parser source are stronger than Discord explanation for implementation claims.
Reddit uses HSV framing; parser/ADR/Discord use HSL or HLS wording. A future canonical model should name the source-specific terminology.
Do not add exact palette values or formulas to data/color-hues.json from this inventory alone.
Observed pale is a separate boolean field; rendering behavior still needs parser/contract confirmation before canonicalization.
Observed snapshot has black/white special values; Reddit provides explanatory categorization, not final canonical policy.
Article explains 8-color meaning; contract/ABI surfaces support uint8[24] output shape. Exact color semantics still need a focused contract/source review.
Do not infer exact accessory color formulas or palettes from this inventory.
ADR 0017 now gives a stronger source for future boundary work, but data/color-hues.json was intentionally left unchanged in this pass.

unresolvedSources.neededFor: Raw provenance for the 2024-01-06 Discord blurb.
unresolvedSources.notes: Only the user-supplied summary was available in this pass.

unresolvedSources.neededFor: Exact on-chain derivation, edge-case mapColors values, and palette semantics.
unresolvedSources.notes: Existing KB has contract/source identity and function surface summaries only.

unresolvedSources.neededFor: Exact parser palette behavior beyond the small reviewed helper surface.
unresolvedSources.notes: The parser snapshot was inspected narrowly; no source code or generated outputs were imported.

agentGuidance: Load this file before changing data/color-hues.json or claiming canonical MoonCat color behavior.
Use ADR and parser/on-chain sources for implementation evidence; use Reddit, Medium, and Discord explanations as interpretation aids with their recorded limits.
Do not promote exact hue ranges, palette formulas, or per-cat color mappings from this inventory without a focused color-model pass.
Preserve HSL/HSV terminology differences instead of normalizing them silently.
limitations: This file is not a canonical color model.
No code snippets, parser tables, ABI JSON, images, article bodies, Reddit threads, Discord messages, exact palettes, or per-cat mappings are imported.
The user-supplied Discord blurb is high confidence by attribution but not independently URL-verified here.
data/color-hues.json remains unchanged by this source inventory pass.
todos: Use this inventory to plan a focused color-model update to data/color-hues.json.
Confirm pale rendering behavior against parser and/or on-chain source before canonicalizing.
If exact hue ranges are promoted, cite ADR 0017 and reconcile them with observed hueName/hueInt snapshot ranges.
Review MoonCatColors source internals before importing edge-case mapColors or exact palette semantics.
